scientific_taste.description: Fourth mechanical PASS in batch. Pattern continues: when content_words include hyphenated/space-separated specific phrases (e.g. 'LLM agent'), they fail to substring-match common literature variants (e.g. 'LLM-guided', 'LLM Multi-Agent'). The strict-substring rule produces PASS when substantive prior art is dense.

honest_note: FOURTH mechanical PASS. All four (R045, R046, R047, R050) are strict-substring artifacts. The pattern is now data: when v2 content_words include LLM-side specific phrases, strict-substring matching is fragile to word-order and morphological variants, producing false-PASS verdicts despite substantive prior art.
